stance: Conservative risk-on within the hedged Equity-Savings mandate: tilt the equity sleeve toward where analyst-revision, momentum and value align — overweight Energy/Materials cyclicals and high-revision Financials; cut refuted laggards.
experience_note: Pre-registered, anti-hindsight. I am watching three things into the next grade: (1) whether the Energy/Materials cyclical cluster's strong revisions + cheapness actually convert into relative outperformance, or whether it was a crowded commodity bounce that fades — if Energy+Materials as a block underperform NIFTY 500 over the hold, the multi-force thesis is wrong and I de-risk the cluster; (2) conviction-IC — did my biggest sizes (Energy/Materials cyclicals, high-revision Financials) actually outrank my smaller ones; if not, my sizing discipline failed even if direction was right; (3) the EXITs — if names I dropped on falling revisions (Tata Motors CV/PV, Adani Ports, RBL, Tata Power, UltraTech, Vodafone Idea) outperform what I added, the revision/momentum signal was mis-weighted this round. I will NOT re-chase any exited name next round unless its revisions turn convincingly positive AND momentum confirms. Since this is an early round with no review to learn from, I have kept the book diversified (40 names, sector-capped) rather than making one big bet.
